substrate_condition: clean with scattered duckweed fronds accumulating near filter outflow
concerns: Dense duckweed coverage may reduce surface gas exchange if left unmanaged
Duckweed accumulation near the filter outflow/powerhead zone — worth monitoring to ensure intake isn't obstructed over time
narrative: This top-down shot of Media Luna on Day 32 of the nitrogen cycle tells a vivid botanical story: hundreds of bright lime-green duckweed fronds now carpet the surface, densely enough to create a nearly continuous living mat with small open-water windows. A single darker, rounder floating plant — likely a small moss ball or a cluster of a larger floater — sits near the midpoint of the tank, while the powerhead or filter return is churning vigorously in the lower-center, creating a clear turbulence zone that's kept duckweed pushed toward the glass edges. Two slim black light fixtures flank the tank on either side, and the dark substrate below is visible through the gaps in coverage — it reads clean and undisturbed.
algae_description: No filamentous or wall algae visible from this angle; the green coverage is entirely duckweed floating at the surface.
